item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is a pretty late reply but the actual answer is 3. people who are really into wine are just kind of weird.

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. The author may revert at any time.

+ show block
– hide block

Honestly, the Alagadda link is pretty perfect, so there is little for me to suggest here outside maybe an in-line link to the MC&D hub. As such, I am suggesting the following:

Editing this:

On 13 October 2007, the first known instance of SCP-3573 was recovered from the home of Enzo Fiorentino, an Italian art collector with known ties to Marshall, Carter, and Dark LLP. Fiorentino had been reported missing by his secretary a few days previously; Foundation agents in the Carabinieri were sent to his villa outside Palermo to investigate, and discovered Fiorentino's body on the floor of his study, holding an instance of SCP-3573. The cause of death was determined to be the total transfiguration of his blood into wine. A search of Fiorentino's home uncovered several documents relevant to the anomaly, as well as a case of empty non-anomalous wine bottles with labels identical to those on SCP-3573 instances.

To this:

On 13 October 2007, the first known instance of SCP-3573 was recovered from the home of Enzo Fiorentino, an Italian art collector with known ties to Marshall, Carter, and Dark LLP. Fiorentino had been reported missing by his secretary a few days previously; Foundation agents in the Carabinieri were sent to his villa outside Palermo to investigate, and discovered Fiorentino's body on the floor of his study, holding an instance of SCP-3573. The cause of death was determined to be the total transfiguration of his blood into wine. A search of Fiorentino's home uncovered several documents relevant to the anomaly, as well as a case of empty non-anomalous wine bottles with labels identical to those on SCP-3573 instances.

item.title: Should've Read the Instruction Manual
item.content:encoded: A simple SCP, and a lovely ending. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooh, was this by any chance inspired by an interaction of Alagadda and MC&D?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seeing that Foundation staff praise a wine for is subtle undertones of blood and books, it brings to mind two possibilities:

1. There's some sort of effect that the wine has on the drinker; possibly an increased likelihood of enjoying it, maybe a memetic effect that puts the idea of those tastes into their mind.

2. The people who tasted the wine are horrible, horrible people.

The two aren't mutually exclusive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love how the concepts of anomalous wine bottles and alchemy are combined here. I admittedly wasn't expecting it to be a light read, but it ended up way darker than I thought, and I enjoyed the straightforward, punchy delivery here.

Excellent work! +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is horrific, disturbing, cruel, darkly humorous, and contains that wonderful pang of dramatic irony at the end that ties it all together beautifully. Brilliant worldbuilding in an unassuming package, and one of the best MC&D articles I've seen to date. Very well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh this is just nice from start to finish, and the details really make it. makes me thorsty tho.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alagadda? MC&D? Arcane powers being used to create something that's equal parts decadent and horrifying? Squee!

+1

Not only am I upvoting, but I'm adding this to my list of personal faves and it will probably show up in Dread & Circuses at some point (Ruprecht Carter would love this stuff).

My only criticism is that the trademark at the end of the title on the series IV list is more of a Wondertainment thing than Marshall, Carter and Dark.

In regards to the idea of whether or not these are actually from Alagadda, I would like to point out that it is in established in A Wandsman in the Court of the Hanged King (By Metaphysician creator of Alagadda himself) that The Deathless Merchant of London (Darke, in my Canon anyway) had been to Alagadda, so it's at least plausible that MC&D could sell genuine Alagadda merchandise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I always love it when an MCD piece makes them feel like a real business while simultaneously portraying their inate horror and absurdity. It’s a difficult balance to strike, but you did a great job of it here. +1

item.title: Nice.
item.content:encoded:
Gotta say, personal favourite of Season 4 so far.
Nice base concept, being a wine bottle that turns blood into wine.

Needless to say, it's like a slightly more sadistic version of that "water-to-wine" stuff.
And, that concept was displayed well.
Bravo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Foundation sommeliers have praised the wine's quality, and identified notes of blood, dried roses, mold and old books.

#1: The Foundation has sommeliers.

#2: You wrote an article where this line is a perfectly reasonable thing to include in the description. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Foundation sommeliers

I can't decide if this is the best foundation job, or a death sentence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought this was decent when I read it, but I like it more after coming to the belated realisation it's basically 'just' a thing that kills you. Like how SCP-3199 is a successful example of 'just' a scary monster, it's a good example of how Series I-style magic objects are still perfectly fine with a bit of nuance and worldbuilding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is cool. All the heavy worldbuilding implications of Series III; all of the cut-and-dry, to-the-point body horror of Series I. We need more of these.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one is dedicated to my stepdad, who loves wine and hates reading instruction manuals.

Image Credit: public domain image from here, modified by me.

Thanks to ModernMajorGeneral in chat for reading over this!

Spoilers/Commentary
– hide block

I honestly haven't decided whether these wine bottles actually come from Alagadda or that's the marketing angle MC&D have decided to use. I might write a MCD-format article that explains that, either as the result of a (possibly millenia-long) trade agreement with one of the Masked Lords or as a clever marketing trick to spin some boring old blood rituals into something more exotic.

Originally this actually was going to be about a millenia-old relationship between MCD and Alagadda, but then Enzo appeared and I decided to cut all the ancient stuff about transport amphorae and ancient Roman religion. Thanks Enzo.

– hide block
